factors.summary: Demand is accelerating, supported by organic revenue growth across all segments.

evidence.text: Net revenues for the three months ended March 31, 2023 increased by 14.8% as compared to the three months ended March 31, 2022. The components of revenue growth for the three months ended March 31, 2023 were as follows: Application Software Network Software Technology Enabled Products Roper Total Revenue Growth 21.2  % 4.7  % 13.0  % 14.8  %
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2023 compared to three months ended March 31, 2022

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is accelerating with strong organic growth across all segments.

factors.summary: Backlog is growing, with organic growth contributing to the increase.

evidence.text: Backlog increased 17% to $2,872.5 at March 31, 2023 as compared to $2,455.6 at March 31, 2022. Organic growth in backlog was 13% and acquisitions contributed 5% which was partially offset by foreign exchange impact of 1%.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2023 compared to three months ended March 31, 2022

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Backlog increased significantly, driven by organic growth.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, benefiting the company.

evidence.text: In our Application Software segment, revenues were $761.4 in the three months ended March 31, 2023 as compared to $628.2 in the three months ended March 31, 2022. The growth of 5.9% in organic revenues was broad-based across the segment led by our businesses serving government contracting, higher education, legal and property and casualty insurance markets.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2023 compared to three months ended March 31, 2022

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, benefiting the company.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, driven by strong performance across all segments.

evidence.text: Net revenues for the three months ended March 31, 2023 increased by 14.8% as compared to the three months ended March 31, 2022. The components of revenue growth for the three months ended March 31, 2023 were as follows: Application Software Network Software Technology Enabled Products Roper Total Revenue Growth 21.2  % 4.7  % 13.0  % 14.8  %
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2023 compared to three months ended March 31, 2022

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is accelerating across all segments.

factors.summary: End markets are growing fastest in government contracting, higher education, legal, property and casualty insurance, freight match, life insurance, and alternate site healthcare.

evidence.text: In our Application Software segment, revenues were $761.4 in the three months ended March 31, 2023 as compared to $628.2 in the three months ended March 31, 2022. The growth of 5.9% in organic revenues was broad-based across the segment led by our businesses serving government contracting, higher education, legal and property and casualty insurance markets.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2023 compared to three months ended March 31, 2022

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: End markets are growing in key sectors, indicating strong demand.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are compressing slightly due to factors like headcount increases and revenue mix.

evidence.text: Gross margin decreased to 68.4% in the three months ended March 31, 2023 as compared to 69.3% in the three months ended March 31, 2022 due primarily to headcount to support growth, and a higher mix of SaaS and professional service revenue across a number of businesses.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2023 compared to three months ended March 31, 2022

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Gross margins are compressing due to headcount increases and revenue mix.

factors.summary: Management appears confident, as evidenced by increased backlog and revenue growth.

evidence.text: Backlog increased 17% to $2,872.5 at March 31, 2023 as compared to $2,455.6 at March 31, 2022. Organic growth in backlog was 13% and acquisitions contributed 5% which was partially offset by foreign exchange impact of 1%.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2023 compared to three months ended March 31, 2022

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management confidence is evident with increased backlog and revenue growth.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include geopolitical and economic uncertainties such as inflation, supply chain disruptions, and labor shortages.

evidence.text: Current geopolitical and economic uncertainties, including the current inflationary environment, supply chain disruptions and labor shortages, could adversely affect our business prospects.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include geopolitical and economic uncertainties.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategy is focused on acquiring high-value businesses and driving organic growth.

evidence.text: We pursue consistent and sustainable growth in revenue, earnings and cash flow by enabling continuous improvement in the operating performance of our businesses and by acquiring other businesses that offer high value-added software, services, technology-enabled products and solutions that we believe are capable of achieving growth and maintaining high margins.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is pursuing growth through acquisitions and organic improvement, indicating a positive outlook.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include economic conditions, supply chain disruptions, and labor shortages.

evidence.text: Current geopolitical and economic uncertainties, including the current inflationary environment, supply chain disruptions and labor shortages, could adversely affect our business prospects.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Economic and supply chain uncertainties pose risks to business prospects.

factors.summary: The company has faced material litigation, such as the settlement of the Berall v. Verathon patent litigation matter.

evidence.text: Other expense, net, of $2.3 for the three months ended March 31, 2023 was composed primarily of foreign exchange losses at our non-U.S. based subsidiaries. Other expense, net, of $2.1 for the three months ended March 31, 2022 was composed primarily of a one-time charge associated with a transaction to transfer the remainder of our exposure related to asbestos claims within the Indicor parameter to a third party, partially offset by foreign exchange gains at our non-U.S. based subsidiaries.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2023 compared to three months ended March 31, 2022

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: A material one-time charge related to litigation indicates financial strain.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are expected to remain stable as a percentage of revenues.

evidence.text: We expect the aggregate of capital expenditures and capitalized software expenditures for the balance of the year to be comparable to prior years as a percentage of revenues.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2023 compared to three months ended March 31, 2022

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Capital expenditures are stable, showing no clear trend.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is stable, with net debt as a percentage of total capital improving.

evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2023 compared to three months ended March 31, 2022

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improvement in net debt to total capital ratio suggests better financial health.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving, driven by higher net income and cash provided by operating activities.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities from continuing operations increased by 5% to $464.9 in the three months ended March 31, 2023 as compared to $441.3 in the three months ended March 31, 2022, due to higher net income from continuing operations net of non-cash expenses.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2023 compared to three months ended March 31, 2022

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Increase in cash flow indicates strong financial performance.

factors.summary: Management maintains an active acquisition program, though future acquisitions are dependent on various factors.

evidence.text: We maintain an active acquisition program; however, future acquisitions will be dependent on numerous factors and it is not feasible to reasonably estimate if or when any such acquisitions will occur and what the impact will be on our business, financial condition and results of operations.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Active acquisition program suggests growth opportunities, despite uncertainties.

factors.summary: Overall economic conditions are uncertain, with risks including inflation, supply chain disruptions, and geopolitical factors.

evidence.text: Current geopolitical and economic uncertainties, including the current inflationary environment, supply chain disruptions and labor shortages, could adversely affect our business prospects.

factors.summary: Geopolitical risks such as conflicts and sanctions pose material risks to the company.

evidence.text: Current geopolitical and economic uncertainties, including the current inflationary environment, supply chain disruptions and labor shortages, could adversely affect our business prospects.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: The evidence highlights significant geopolitical and economic uncertainties that could materially harm business prospects.
